topic: A person finds their childhood home listed for sale online
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed on my screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into the dull rhythm of my afternoon. My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming just beneath my ribs. It was a picture of the house—my childhood sanctuary—with a price tag plastered across the listing. I stared at the image, my jaw tightening until my molars ached. How could this happen? Someone else? The thought tasted like ash in my mouth. I slammed my laptop shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet apartment. I walked to the window, the glass cool against my forehead, and looked out at the street, feeling a sudden, sharp surge of resentment towards the world that had dared to put my past up for auction.

[story 2]

I clicked the link again, my fingers hovering over the screen as if afraid to touch the surface. A wave of nausea washed over me. The listing details were precise, the layout familiar yet alien, every faded paint swatch and overgrown rose bush a painful trigger. I paced the cramped living room, my breath coming in shallow, rapid gasps. "No," I muttered to the empty room, the sound rough and strained. It felt like a deliberate dismantling, as if someone were trying to erase the memory of where I started. I scrubbed a hand over my face, feeling the heat rise in my cheeks, a burning frustration that wouldn't subside. This intrusion felt like a violation, a deliberate attempt to strip me of something irreplaceable.

[story 3]

The email had landed, and I didn't bother reading the fine print. My eyes skimmed the photos, and a low growl started deep in my chest. Every familiar corner, every chipped piece of molding, screamed of a history I was suddenly unwelcome to touch. I stood rigid, my hands balling into fists at my sides. I wanted to delete the page, to erase the digital ghost of that place. A bitter, acidic feeling bubbled up, making my throat constrict. I spun around, pacing the length of the hallway, my movements jerky and sharp, a caged animal vibrating with suppressed fury at the audacity of it all.
